title: Override "write(byte[],int,int)" in subclasses of java.io.OutputStream or java.io.FilterOutputStream
description: When directly subclassing java.io.OutputStream or java.io.FilterOutputStream, it is strongly recommended that subclasses provide an efficient implementation of write(byte[],int,int). This rule raises an issue when a direct subclass of java.io.OutputStream or java.io.FilterOutputStream doesn't provide an override of write(byte[],int,int).
recommendation: Provide an efficient implementation of write(byte[],int,int) in the subclass.
impact: Can create a lot of overhead and is utterly inefficient.

checks.message: Provide an efficient implementation of write(byte[],int,int) in the subclass.
